article_title: Relacorilant Becomes the First Glucocorticoid Receptor Antagonist Approved for Ovarian Cancer as Full ROSELLA Data Land in The Lancet

content_preview.title: Relacorilant Becomes the First Glucocorticoid Receptor Antagonist Approved for Ovarian Cancer as Full ROSELLA Data Land in The Lancet
content_preview.summary: Corcept's relacorilant plus nab-paclitaxel cut the risk of death 35% in platinum-resistant ovarian cancer, raising median survival to 16 months from 11.9, with the phase 3 ROSELLA results published in The Lancet.
content_preview.body_excerpt:
## Overview

The phase 3 ROSELLA trial has established a new option for platinum-resistant ovarian cancer, one of the deadliest gynecologic malignancies. Adding the oral drug relacorilant to chemotherapy raised median overall survival to 16 months from 11.9 months and cut the risk of death by 35%, according to results presented at the 2026 Society of Gynecologic Oncology (SGO) Annual Meeting on Women's Cancer and published in The Lancet, [as reported by The ASCO Post](https://ascopost.com/news/m...

editor_notes.content_quality: Well-structured News piece (680 words, in range) using the standard Overview / What We Know / Why It Matters / What We Don't Know format. Clear, precise reporting of the ROSELLA phase 3 data with appropriate clinical detail.
editor_notes.source_verification: All 5 source snapshots read from disk and verified. source-3 (ASCO Post, source-3.html.gz) confirms median OS 16.0 vs 11.9 months, HR 0.65, P=.0004, 35% risk-of-death reduction, SGO 2026 + The Lancet, 381 patients 1:1, 150 mg, and the Olawaiye ('very difficult to treat, with a short median overall survival') and O'Cearbhaill ('a biologically rational study') quotes verbatim. source-0 (CancerNetwork FDA-approval, source-0.html.gz) confirms PFS 6.5 (CI 5.6-7.4) vs 5.5 (CI 3.9-5.9), HR 0.70 (CI 0.54-0.91, P=.0076); OS CIs 13.0-18.3 and 10.0-13.8 with HR 0.65 (CI 0.51-0.83, P=.0004); the adverse-reaction list; the FDA indication (epithelial ovarian/fallopian tube/primary peritoneal, 1-3 prior regimens including bevacizumab); and the Coleman quote 'fills a great need for us in that patient population.' source-1 (source-1.html.gz) confirms the taxane-free-interval subgroups: 16.7 vs 11.0 (HR 0.60, CI 0.31-1.15) and 15.7 vs 12.1 (HR 0.66, CI 0.51-0.86). source-2 (source-2.html.gz) confirms the mechanism description (oral, selective GR antagonist modulating cortisol via receptor binding). source-4 (BioSpace Corcept release, source-4.html.gz) confirms the Lucy Gilbert title (Director of Gynecologic Oncology, McGill University Health Centre) and her quote verbatim, the 'first FDA-approved selective glucocorticoid receptor antagonist' claim, the Lifyorli brand, and the 'March 2026' approval timing.
editor_notes.factual_accuracy: Every number, name, code, and date traces to a cited source. Rule 9 scrutiny applied: the headline OS specifics (16/11.9, HR 0.65, P=.0004) are carried by ASCO Post (the cited outlet for that sentence) and independently corroborated in CancerNetwork source-0 and source-1; PFS specifics (6.5/5.5, HR 0.70, P=.0076) are correctly cited to CancerNetwork source-0. The approval is hedged to 'March 2026' with NO specific day stated (sources disagreed: CancerNetwork dated the article March 25, the Corcept/BioSpace release says only 'March 2026'); the article correctly used the conservative hedge. The Lucy Gilbert quote is verbatim against source-4. No hallucinations detected.
editor_notes.tone_assessment: Neutral and professional. No sensationalism, no editorializing, no AI self-reference. Limitations honestly stated in the 'What We Don't Know' section.
editor_notes.originality: Original topic — no prior relacorilant/ROSELLA/Corcept/Lifyorli coverage in the archive. Distinct from the Intellia in-vivo CRISPR HAE story (2026-06/18) and the Utebzi oral-carbapenem antibiotic story (2026-06/19): different drugs, diseases, and mechanisms. The cross-reference to the February 2026 Keytruda platinum-resistant-ovarian-cancer article resolves to a real published article (2026-02/28-keytruda-...) and is a legitimate developing-story editorial link (same disease setting, contrasting mechanism).
editor_notes.overall_assessment: High-quality, fully sourced News submission. All integrity checks pass, signature valid, every claim verified against the cited snapshots, quotes verbatim, approval timing correctly hedged, cross-reference resolves, and the topic is original. Approved for publication.
